stem: The function \( f(x) = \begin{cases} [[a]]x + ([[b]]) & x < [[c]] \\ [[d]]x + k & x \ge [[c]] \end{cases} \) is continuous for all real \( x \). Find the value of the constant \( k \).
solution: Continuity at the junction \( x = [[c]] \) requires the pieces to agree there: \( [[a]]\cdot[[c]] + ([[b]]) = [[d]]\cdot[[c]] + k \). Solving for \( k \) gives \( k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the term \( d\,c \) instead of subtracting it when isolating \( k \).
This reverses the slope difference, giving \( (d-a)c + b \).
This flips the sign of the constant \( b \).
This assumes \( k \) simply equals the intercept \( b \).
This forgets to include the intercept \( b \).
This is off by one from the correct constant.

stem: The function \( f(x) = \begin{cases} [[a]]x + ([[b]]) & x < [[c]] \\ kx + ([[d]]) & x \ge [[c]] \end{cases} \) is continuous for all real \( x \), where \( k \) is the (unknown) slope of the second piece. Find \( k \).
solution: Continuity at \( x = [[c]] \) requires \( [[a]]\cdot[[c]] + ([[b]]) = k\cdot[[c]] + ([[d]]) \). Isolating the unknown slope: \( k = \dfrac{[[a]]\cdot[[c]] + ([[b]]) - ([[d]])}{[[c]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the unknown slope just equals the known slope \( a \).
This adds \( d \) instead of subtracting it before dividing by \( c \).
This drops the first piece's contribution entirely.
This forgets to divide by \( c \) when isolating the slope.
This has the wrong sign on the numerator before dividing.
This is off by one from the correct slope.

stem: The function \( f(x) = \begin{cases} [[a]]x^{2} + ([[b]]) & x < [[c]] \\ [[d]]x + k & x \ge [[c]] \end{cases} \) is continuous for all real \( x \). Find the value of the constant \( k \).
solution: Continuity at \( x = [[c]] \) requires the quadratic piece and the linear piece to agree there: \( [[a]]\cdot[[c]]^{2} + ([[b]]) = [[d]]\cdot[[c]] + k \). Solving for \( k \) gives \( k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( c \) instead of \( c^{2} \) in the quadratic term.
This adds \( dc \) instead of subtracting it when isolating \( k \).
This flips the sign of the constant \( b \).
This drops the quadratic term \( ac^{2} \) entirely.
This doubles the quadratic term's contribution, as if differentiating it.
This is off by one from the correct constant.

stem: The function \( f(x) = \begin{cases} kx + ([[b]]) & x < [[c]] \\ [[a]]x + k & x \ge [[c]] \end{cases} \) is continuous for all real \( x \), where the same constant \( k \) appears in both pieces. Find \( k \).
solution: Continuity requires \( k\cdot[[c]] + ([[b]]) = [[a]]\cdot[[c]] + k \). Collecting the \( k \) terms: \( k([[c]] - 1) = [[a]]\cdot[[c]] - ([[b]]) \), so \( k = \dfrac{[[a]]\cdot[[c]] - ([[b]])}{[[c]] - 1} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This divides by \( c - 1 \) but omits subtracting \( b \) correctly in the numerator sign.
This adds 1 instead of subtracting 1 in the denominator.
This ignores the equation entirely and just subtracts the constants.
This isolates \( k \) as if it appeared only once, dividing by \( c \) instead of \( c-1 \).
This has the wrong sign on the whole expression.
This is off by one from the correct constant.

stem: The function \( f(x) = \begin{cases} [[a]]x + ([[b]]) & x < [[p]] \\ kx + m & [[p]] \le x < [[q]] \\ [[d]]x + ([[e]]) & x \ge [[q]] \end{cases} \) is continuous for all real \( x \). Find the value of the constant \( k \).
solution: Matching the middle piece to its neighbors at both junctions gives the system \( [[a]]\cdot[[p]] + ([[b]]) = k\cdot[[p]] + m \) and \( k\cdot[[q]] + m = [[d]]\cdot[[q]] + ([[e]]) \). Solving this \( 2\times 2 \) system for \( k \) (eliminating \( m \)) gives \( k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the middle slope just equals the first piece's slope.
This assumes the middle slope just equals the third piece's slope.
This averages the two outer slopes instead of solving the system.
This has the numerator's sign flipped in the elimination step.
This has the denominator's sign flipped in the elimination step.
This is off by one from the correct constant.
